methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $318M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Svante is a Series E climate and energy company founded by Claude Letourneau that has raised $318M to date. The comparable universe includes one public peer (LNZA) and eight private companies in the Climate & Energy sector, including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. A narrow peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage comparables within the Climate & Energy sector; only two adjacent-stage peers were identified, falling short of the three-datapoint threshold required for statistical reliability.

Stage-progression analysis was the primary applicable method. Using total capital raised ($318M) as a valuation proxy for the current round, a 1.4x multiplier reflecting typical Series E to next-stage step-ups implies a valuation of $445M. This method reflects observed progression patterns across similar-stage companies but relies on a single quantitative anchor.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $378M (low) to $512M (high), with a median of $445M. This relatively narrow band reflects reliance on a single methodology rather than broad consensus across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties stem from the scarcity of directly comparable Climate & Energy companies at Series E maturity and the inherent variability in step-up multiples across funding rounds. Material value drivers—including revenue trajectory, capital efficiency, and market adoption rates—remain outside the scope of this capital-structure-based analysis and warrant separate operational due diligence.
